items.title: GST push to follow SA budget, treasurer says

children.content: South Australia's treasurer has foreshadowed a push to secure GST revenue, calling on MPs across the political spectrum to step up their advocacy efforts — but the opposition has questioned why a recent GST windfall is not being used to counter soaring debt.

articles.title: No surprises but risks aplenty in Tom Koutsantonis's comeback budget

children.content: Some things haven't changed since Labor Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis last handed down a state budget in 2017.

image.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and blue tie sits in a red chair in front of a window holding a booklet that says State Budget 2026-27

articles.title: Big spending on health and housing in 'no surprises' SA budget

children.content: South Australia's debt is projected to hit to more than $50 billion by 2029-30, with health taking up about one third of the budget.

image.altText: Tom Koutsantonis is standing behind a lecturn and speaking into a microphone.

articles.title: Are you a winner or loser in this year's SA budget?

children.content: South Australian Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis has unveiled the 2026-27 state budget. Here are the winners and losers.

items.title: Future of iconic wine Wolf Blass unclear amid large brand overhaul

children.content: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

image.altText: a red wine label

items.title: Public servant claims threat of $4m damages bill in golf course fight

children.content: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Body-in-suitcase killer loses bid to reduce sentence for double murder

children.content: The NSW Court of Criminal Appeal dismisses a bid by Daniel Holdom to have his sentence reduced after he pleaded guilty in 2018 to murdering his girlfriend and her two-year-old daughter before dumping the child's body in a suitcase on the side of a remote highway.

gallery.altText: Khandalyce Kiara Pearce and Karlie Jade Pearce-Stevenson

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash

children.content: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Honouring the man who kept cinema alive in remote outback city

children.content: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

image.altText: A black and white photograph of a man dressed up as Rambo, posing with a gun

items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

image.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

items.title: 'Sick and tired': Northern Adelaide hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours

children.content: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

image.altText: A lady in a black t-shirt stands with arms folded defiantly out front of the entrance to Lyell McEwin Hospital

items.title: Full Circle launches professional musical theatre company in Adelaide

children.content: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

image.altText: Woman sits on a chair in a garden

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

image.altText: a generic photo of a wheat crop

items.title: 'Too dangerous to push ahead': Aussie diver leaves Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

image.altText: A man with a beard and cap in a jungle camp

items.title: This is the 17th Ebola outbreak in 50 years. But this one is different

children.content: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

items.title: Bronze statue immortalises Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue

children.content: A bronze statue of influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue has been unveiled in Adelaide — the first of six monuments of prominent Indigenous South Australians that the state government has planned.

image.altText: A  bronze statue of a woman who holds her hands at the front of her body.

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

image.altText: Young women leans on her kitchen bench, posing for photo

images.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

articles.title: ASIC has 8 contracts with KPMG despite audit scandal, ASX loses ground — as it happened

children.content: ASIC tells Senate estimates it has eight active contracts with KPMG, despite the company facing an audit scandal. Meanwhile, the ASX lost ground. Catch up on the day's events and insights in our live markets blog.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, sitting at a desk between other people.

articles.title: What happens when fuel excise cuts end on June 30?

children.content: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

articles.title: Hanson takes the stage as budget woes roll on

children.content: While Labor is increasingly concerned about the long-term threat of One Nation, this week its attention was squarely on more immediate problems.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon and white striped tie.

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government's tax reforms are proof the government is "not here to simply occupy the space or pass the time". Follow live.

items.title: PM says he won't engage in 'drawn out' changes to tax reform plan

updates.title: Rise in One Nation support context for budget decisions, PM says
PM says govt won't engage in 'long, drawn out' changes to budget legislation
Albanese says government has no intention of 'wringing our hands'

items.title: Rumours, rifts and a reported resignation form a murky picture of a war-ravaged Iran

children.content: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

image.altText: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian delivers a speech.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: Ancient tribes, military, trade and China's rise meet on this island

children.content: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: Doctor in tears over 'atypical' moles as social media craze causes concern

children.content: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

image.altText: Large moles on a young person's back.

items.title: Qualifier makes French Open history, reaching final against Russian teen star

children.content: Nineteen-year-old Mirra Andreeva reaches her first grand slam final at the French Open where she will take on Poland's Maja Chwalińska, the first qualifier to reach the Paris decider in the Open Era.

image.altText: Maja Chwalinska puts her hand on her head at the French Open.

children.content: Hannah and Garrett's steamy love story might be on ice, but we've found more romance and yearning to tick your comfort-viewing boxes.

items.title: A new documentary brings things home for the author of Dark Emu

children.content: A slow-cinema treasure, Grace McKenzie's documentary, Yumburra, traces author Bruce Pascoe's establishment of a farm.

items.title: Experts suspect state policy is putting some farmers in artificial drought

children.content: A western New South Wales farmer says dams built next door have stopped all water flows onto his property, forcing him to graze and water his stock by the side of the road.

articles.title: Farmers are increasingly embracing change, and it's bringing them hope

children.content: When grazier Matthew Peart took over his family's organic beef operation almost 20 years ago, his pastures were rundown, and the future looked bleak. But now, he's unashamedly optimistic, and a new report suggests he's not alone.

image.altText: Mid  shot from behind of farmer leaning backwards against the front of a ute looking out to a sunset

articles.title: Hot spot for Australian bull semen as China market reopens

children.content: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

image.altText: Photo of vials of frozen bull semen.

items.title: Zelenskyy writes open letter inviting Putin to talks to end Ukraine war

children.content: An open letter is the first public message Mr Zelenskyy has written directly to Mr Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four

children.content: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

items.title: Portugal and Austria defeat Germany for UN Security Council seats

children.content: Portugal and Austria have defeated Germany for seats on the powerful but deeply divided UN Security Council in a hotly contested race after intense campaigning.

image.altText: The symbol of the United Nations - a world map and olive branches - is displayed on the side of high-rise building.

items.title: Sex discrimination commissioner slams gender definition proposal

children.content: Australia's sex discrimination commissioner condemns a proposal to reinstate biological definition of sexes as a "step back" for women.

image.altText: Politician speaks in Canberra with Nationals leader in background.

items.title: Tantric workshops, couple swaps and toys: Intimacy pressures on MAFS exposed

children.content: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

items.title: Socceroo O'Neill shakes off injury scare to be fit for World Cup debut

children.content: Aiden O'Neill put a scare through the Socceroos camp when he went down with an ankle injury in early May, but says he is fit and ready for his World Cup debut.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill of the Socceroos defends against Cameroon.

items.title: Public apology after Nepal's women's national team violates 'spirit of cricket'

children.content: Nepal's cricket board has offered a public apology after its women's team successfully had a batter timed out.

items.title: Bullock says RBA will re-tender KPMG whistleblower hotline contract amid scandals

children.content: The Reserve Bank is distancing itself from KPMG with Governor Michele Bullock confirming the bank will re-tender its whistleblower hotline service.

items.title: CCTV vision shows teens' brutal attack on homeless man

children.content: Police say the man stumbled into a sports bar with stab wounds and collapsed.

items.title: Testing underway after mass bird death in wheatbelt

children.content: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.
